evidence.role: Current employee - Lead Product Manager - Technical
evidence.text: Excellent 401K match, great PTO but it is in too many buckets (sick, vacation, and personal) that do not roll over. Amazing Paid Parental Leave. Great Benefits but could be improved
Great culture and some of the best benefits in the world. Fantastic benefits, good pay, good career growth.
Good pay and friendly coworkers Good pay, poor balance
Good benefits and great coworkers. Good benefits and bonus, low base and slow work
Excellent pay and time off, insurance could be better Great pay and benefits
Smart, driven people and great company policies (e.g., PTO, parental leave) that supports a good work life balance. Great work life balance
Work-life balance 401k matching Flexible time off Good work-life balance, but technology not well managed
Good work life balance and benefits Good work life balance

evidence.role: Current employee - Senior Big Data Engineer
evidence.text: Best place for Work Life balance
Very great work life balance Great work life balance, low upward mobility

evidence.role: Current employee - Senior Business Analyst/Project Manager
evidence.text: Mastercard offers a collaborative environment where individual contributions are acknowledged and valued. Throughout my tenure, I have participated in meaningful projects and received recognition for both results and teamwork. Dedicating extra time for testing or providing support to colleagues during challenging periods exemplifies the emphasis Mastercard places on accountability, empathy, and high standards. For p...

Many talented and intelligent employees who are collaborative and willing to help each other succeed. The benefits package is very competitive, and depending on the team, work-life balance can be quite good. The company also provides strong learning opportunities, especially for those interested in growing their skills. There are mentorship programs and development resources available for employees who take initiati...
Interesting work, opportunity to work with people in different industries. Disorganized
Good culture, people and nice office. Overall good benefit package. Limited Growth
Amazing PTO policy and a very competitive 401k program. Great Benefits, but Management Transparency Needs Improvement
Great benefits and time off Used to be good

Celebrating 10 years with Mastercard has been an incredible journey. The company has evolved tremendously - from a small, ambitious team to a mature organization with a strong culture of collaboration and innovation. Leadership genuinely cares about employee development, providing continuous opportunities to learn and grow. The work environment encourages creativity, open communication, and teamwork. Benefits and wo...
Used to be a good job security but not anymore. If you are a female or/and not Indian, I would not work here. Unstable work environment

The company fosters a collaborative atmosphere with a large team, allowing employees to gain valuable experience in teamwork and communication. There are always a variety of projects to work on, which keeps the job interesting and provides continuous learning opportunities. A Place for Collaboration and Continuous Learning

Mastercard colleagues are incredibly intelligent, encouraging to others, collaborative with others and conducive to new ideas. I truly love working at mastercard, it's a truly GREAT PLACE TO WORK. Additionally, the benefits are unparalleled. As someone who recently had a baby, having the ability to spend an extended time with my child with paid leave is a luxury i would not have experienced at similar companies. I w...
Very relaxed work pace, understanding managers. Great benefits (167% match on 401k) Chill but lower base salary and you must play office politics to advance
Very ethical management. I loved it when they stood beside their employees during pandemic. Work culture is dynamic and has a focus on growth. Ethical, Dynamic and Driven
They care about their employees first and foremost Lots of mobility within the company Great inclusive place to work
Job security is good. WLB is good. 401k match is great. Just Ok
Supportive environment and good benefits Great place to work

Not a great workplace. No Team culture. People are always insecure with their job and team members. Some people gets promotions just by licking the boots of the higher ups and this creates a bad atmosphere for others working hard. Not a great Workplace
Projects can be stressful sometimes. Great teams, a little stressful
stressful and boring, hard to get promoted, sales staff are valued much more than others Pays Well but stressful and boring
Not the most exciting role; many of the projects are part of broader MC credit card contracts Slow career progression; a "do your time" culture Great benefits, not the most exciting role

evidence.role: Current employee - Principal Technical Program Manager
evidence.text: Long hours at times Can be stressful on occasion Best Place to Work

In my opinion the culture includes extreme reverse racism and toxic feminism driven by Facebook trolls that are retaliating against civil rights violation reporting; via both sexual discrimination and harassment. Senior, middle and low level management believe anything without verifying the facts. Same with false rumors spread amongst the rank-and-file. In my opinion interviewing and onboarding was a complete waste...

P&C have been one of the most toxic teams I've ever worked with. The leadership is delusional and lacks basic social skills. Communication is poor and there is zero transparency. Nepotism and favoritism is a standard practice. People are quietly fired and replaced with friends of the leadership. You could experience bullying and harassment every day. Middle managers use inappropriate language all the time and be...

The increasing favoritism in some teams has led to an extremely toxic work environment to the extent that colleagues not in the "favored few" live in a constant state of fear and dread. Ageism is also blatant for everyone below EVP , so don't plan to retire from here unless you make it to the top. Be prepared to manage up to get promoted - especially in IMC Exhausting politics and favoritism

Where to begin? Mastercard is the poster child for corporate silos where every department is an island and collaboration is more myth than mandate. Want to make an impact (especially in Comms)? Better bring a bullhorn and a selfie stick, because self-promotion is the only currency that matters. Quietly doing great work? No one will notice, especially not the EVPs (Especially in Marketing and Comms) who wonât make...

Widespread issues with unqualified leadership, scapegoating, and retaliation. I experienced multiple instances of peers being scapegoated and retaliated against for management failures or constructive criticism. This is prevalent within the Services division. Poor leadership, retaliation and scapegoating problems

Lack of development, strategic leadership is lacking in the Marketing field, tight turnarounds, limited voice and ability to expectation manage, seems to be a prejudice against those not in Purchase for growth, promotion, development, pay is not great Stable but lacks development plans and strategic leadership

All of my pros may not be consistent across teams. As with most places, bad managers and ICs are hard to root out. Still, leadership & HR are attentive and generally responsive to (legitimate) feedback. About as good as it gets
Not great guidance on promotion Great company, not clear guidance on promotion
- Commute from NYC is a little rough - Didn't have opportunity to WFH much Great company. Solid industry fundamentals

I have worked with some great managers and some not so great managers. My current role it seems like all of the MGMT is more worried about their career growth and the easiest path forward then taking care of the customers. There are a lot of self inflicted impacts that have hit the customer. I have done my best to try and prevent but the MGMT teams seem to either drag their feet or ignore. I have more pride than tha...
do not have cons for me so far good work life balance
Annual hikes are not good Good work life balance
Will not be as much pay as a major tech company but the work life balance and benefits counteract that Good place to work
old tech, tech support isn't great sometimes, pipelines are old. Some upcoming budget issues which is pausing or dissuading hiring Good Work Life Balance
Compensation is not high compared to other financial institutions Great work-life balance
I don't think i have experienced any con's in my sight while being at MasterCard Amazing Experience from an Amazing Company
I was not able to spend more time at this firm. Great Person
You will never earn âa lotâ at Mastercard Perfect Company
I have not found any negatives yet. Super Company to work for
Won't allow you to work full time remote. Only partial (2-3 days from home). Great company to start a career path
